meaning: I'm at maximum capacity / I'm fully focused
communicative_purpose: To express being at full capacity, whether busy, tired, or focused
trigger_situations: Very busy schedules, high focus, maximum effort, exhaustion
contexts: Work overload, sports training, party exhaustion, mental focus
variations: je suis à bloc, je suis à fond, je suis à block, je suis full
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'bloc' meaning physical block; spelling variants
example_1: Mon calendrier est full cette semaine, je suis à bloc.
example_2: Je suis à bloc sur ce projet, deadline demain.
example_3: HS après cette journée de formation, je suis à bloc total.
example_1_translation: My calendar is full this week, I'm full.
example_2_translation: I'm working on this project, deadline tomorrow.
example_3_translation: HS after this day of training, I am completely fired up.

meaning: it's my thing / it's what I love
communicative_purpose: To express that something is what you love most or are passionate about
trigger_situations: Talking about passions, hobbies, personal interests, identity
contexts: Social media bios, friend conversations, personal declarations
variations: c'est mon kiff, c'est mon truc, c'est ma passion, c'est mon dada
common_mistakes: Mixing registers; 'kiff' is casual, 'passion' is more serious
collocates: préféré, absoluto, dans l'âme, pour toujours
example_1: La musique électronique, c'est mon kiff depuis toujours.
example_2: Voyager seul, c'est mon kiff maximum, je kiffe tout dans ce contexte.
example_3: Cuisiner pour mes potes, c'est vraiment mon kiff.
example_1_translation: Electronic music has always been my favorite.
example_2_translation: Traveling alone is my favorite thing, I love everything in this context.
example_3_translation: Cooking for my friends is really my favorite thing.

meaning: we have a problem / there's an issue
communicative_purpose: To announce that there is a problem or issue that needs attention
trigger_situations: Identifying problems, notifying teams, troubleshooting, issues arise
contexts: Work projects, technical issues, group planning, unexpected problems
variations: on a un souci, il y a un souci, on a un problème, y'a un hic
common_mistakes: Confusing 'souci' (worry/problem) with 'soucis' (hair care); being too alarmist
example_1: On a un souci avec la livraison, elle est retardée.
example_2: Il y a un souci au niveau du serveur, le site est down.
example_3: On a un souci de budget pour ce projet, il va falloir ajuster.
example_1_translation: We have a problem with the delivery, it is delayed.
example_2_translation: There is a problem with the server, the site is down.
example_3_translation: We have budget concerns for this project, we will have to adjust.

communicative_purpose: To express being emotionally devastated or in shock
trigger_situations: After emotional events, shocking news, traumatic experiences
contexts: Breakups, deaths, shocking news, emotional trauma, drama
variations: je suis en PLS, je suis en PLS total, je suis détruit, je suis dévasté
common_mistakes: Original meaning (physical positions) changed to emotional trauma in slang
collocates: total, totale, après, à cause de
example_1: J'ai appris la nouvelle ce matin, je suis en PLS.
example_2: Le dernier épisode de la série m'a mis en PLS total.
example_3: Cette rupture m'a laissé en PLS pendant des semaines.
example_1_translation: I heard the news this morning, I'm in PLS.
example_2_translation: The last episode of the series put me in total PLS.
example_3_translation: This breakup left me in PLS for weeks.

communicative_purpose: To describe a chaotic, messy, or disorganized situation
trigger_situations: Messy spaces, chaotic situations, disorganization, chaos
contexts: Offices, homes, events, planning gone wrong, messy situations
variations: c'est le bazar, c'est le boxon, c'est le bordel, c'est le foutoir
common_mistakes: 'Bordel' is more vulgar; 'bazar' is softer but still casual
example_1: Le bureau est un vrai bazar, je sais même plus où ranger les dossiers.
example_2: Cette organisation de mariage, c'est le bazar total.
example_3: Y'a le bazar dans ma tête, j'arrive pas à m'organiser.
example_1_translation: The office is a real mess, I don't even know where to put the files anymore.
example_2_translation: This wedding planning is a total mess.
example_3_translation: There's a mess in my head, I can't organize myself.

meaning: let me know if / tell me if
communicative_purpose: To leave an offer open and invite someone to communicate
trigger_situations: Offering help, leaving options open, checking preferences
contexts: Planning, offers of help, checking availability, invitations
variations: tu me dis, tu me dis si, dis-moi si, n'hésite pas
common_mistakes: Missing 'si' when condition is implied; sounding too pushy
collocates: tu veux, t'as besoin, ça te va, c'est bon pour toi
example_1: Tu veux qu'on sorte ? Tu me dis si ça te dit.
example_2: Tu me dis si t'as besoin d'aide pour les courses.
example_3: N'hésite pas, tu me dis si tu veux venir.
example_1_translation: Do you want to go out? Tell me if you like.
example_2_translation: Tell me if you need help with shopping.
example_3_translation: Don't hesitate, tell me if you want to come.

meaning: I'm in survival mode / I'm barely coping
communicative_purpose: To express being barely able to cope with current demands
trigger_situations: Busy periods, stressful times, difficult circumstances
contexts: Work deadlines, personal crises, overwhelming schedules
variations: je suis en mode survie, je suis en survie, c'est la survie, mode survie
common_mistakes: Using too casually when someone is in real crisis; overusing
example_1: Trois deadlines cette semaine, je suis en mode survie totale.
example_2: Bébé qui fait ses dents, on est en mode survie à la maison.
example_3: Les exams approchent, je passe en mode survie.
example_1_translation: Three deadlines this week, I'm in total survival mode.
example_2_translation: Baby who is teething, we are in survival mode at home.
example_3_translation: The exams are approaching, I go into survival mode.

communicative_purpose: To express that something is great, enjoyable, or approved of
trigger_situations: Approving of things, expressing enjoyment, recommending
contexts: Social media, movies, restaurants, experiences, opinions
variations: c'est kiff, c'est trop kiff, c'est mon kiff, c'est un kiff
common_mistakes: Overusing it until it loses meaning; mixing with more formal expressions
example_1: Ce podcast sur le vrai crime, c'est kiff grave !
example_2: Le concert en plein air, c'était un kiff inoubliable.
example_3: Ta nouvelle coiffure, c'est kiff, ça te va bien !
example_1_translation: This true crime podcast is seriously awesome!
example_3_translation: Your new hairstyle is awesome, it suits you!

meaning: I'm dead / I'm exhausted / I'm out of service
communicative_purpose: To express being extremely tired or done for the day
trigger_situations: After long days, exhaustion, end of energy, done for the day
contexts: After work, school, parties, intense activities, illness
variations: je suis HS, je suis HS total, je suis K-O, je suis cassé
common_mistakes: HS originally means 'hors service' (out of service) - used for objects first
example_1: Après 12 heures de bureau, je suis HS total.
example_2: HS ce soir, on annule, désolé les potes.
example_3: Le marathon d'hier, j'étais HS pendant trois jours.
example_1_translation: After 12 hours at the office, I'm totally out of work.
example_2_translation: HS tonight, we cancel, sorry friends.
example_3_translation: Yesterday's marathon, I was out of work for three days.

meaning: I'm going to ghost / I'm going to disappear
communicative_purpose: To announce plans to disappear or cut contact with someone
trigger_situations: Ending relationships, social cleanup, ignoring people
contexts: Dating gone wrong, toxic relationships, social media, group chats
variations: je vais ftacher, je vais ghoster, je vais ignorer, je vais bloquer
common_mistakes: English word 'ghoster' increasingly used in French; spelling variants
collocates: cette personne, ce groupe, les messages, cette conversation
example_1: Ce mec m'emmerde, je vais ftacher sans avertir.
example_2: Je vais ftacher ce groupe WhatsApp, y'a trop de drama.
example_3: Elle ghostait tout le monde après ses breakups.
example_1_translation: This guy is annoying me, I'm going to piss off without warning.
example_2_translation: I'm going to leave this WhatsApp group, there's too much drama.
example_3_translation: She ghosted everyone after her breakups.

meaning: it's crazy / it's madness / it's awesome
communicative_purpose: To express that something is wild, crazy, or incredibly good
trigger_situations: Amazing events, chaos, excitement, unbelievable situations
contexts: Parties, sales, shocking news, incredible experiences
variations: c'est la folie, c'est absolute folie, c'est la folie totale, c'est too much
common_mistakes: Context needed - can mean crazy good OR crazy bad; clarifying needed
example_1: Les soldes ce matin, c'était la folie devant les boutiques !
example_2: Le concert d'hier soir, c'était la folie, quelle ambiance !
example_3: Le prix de l'essence, c'est la folie en ce moment.
example_1_translation: The sales this morning were crazy in front of the stores!
example_2_translation: Last night's concert was crazy, what an atmosphere!
example_3_translation: The price of gas is crazy right now.

meaning: let's have a drink / let's do drinks
communicative_purpose: To invite people for drinks (alcoholic or not) together
trigger_situations: Casual drinking invitations, after-work drinks, socializing
contexts: Work socializing, friend gatherings, after下班 drinks
variations: on fait un pot, on fait le pot, on va prendre un pot, on se fait un pot
common_mistakes: 'Pot' in this context means a drink (from 'pot de vin' origin), not container
collocates: comme d'hab, ce soir, après le taf, entre nous
example_1: On fait un pot ce soir après le taf ?
example_2: C'est l'heure de l'apéro, on fait un pot ?
example_3: Les nouveaux collègues, on se fait un pot pour faire connaissance ?
example_1_translation: Shall we have a drink this evening after work?
example_2_translation: It's aperitif time, shall we have a drink?
example_3_translation: New colleagues, should we have a drink to get to know each other?

meaning: I can't handle it / I don't manage
communicative_purpose: To express being unable to cope with or handle something
trigger_situations: Overwhelm, stress, inability to cope, difficult situations
contexts: Work stress, emotional overwhelm, technical issues, social pressure
variations: je gère pas, je gère plus, je gère rien, j'arrive pas à gérer
common_mistakes: Mixing tenses; 'je gère pas' (present) vs 'je gérais pas' (past)
example_1: Le kids en pleine crise, je gère plus à cette heure.
example_2: Je gère pas le stress des examens, j'ai les boules.
example_3: Cette situation au bureau, je gère vraiment pas.
example_1_translation: The kids in the middle of a crisis, I can't handle it anymore at this time.
example_2_translation: I can't handle the stress of exams, I'm upset.
example_3_translation: I really can't handle this situation at the office.

meaning: you're messing with me / you're kidding
communicative_purpose: To express that someone is joking or trying to fool you
trigger_situations: Suspicion of jokes, disbelief, catching lies, teasing back
contexts: Friend banter, catching exaggerations, disbelief, playful confrontation
variations: tu me gobe, tu me promènes, tu me prunes, tu blagues
common_mistakes: Regional variations; 'gobé' might not be understood everywhere
example_1: Tu me gobe là ? C'est pas vrai cette histoire ?
example_2: Eh mec, tu me gobe ! Je vais pas gober ça non plus.
example_3: Tu veux me faire croire ça ? Tu me gobes complet.
example_1_translation: Are you swallowing me? Isn't this story true?
example_2_translation: Hey man, you’re swallowing me up! I'm not going to buy that either.
example_3_translation: You want me to believe that? You devour me completely.

meaning: things are going well / it's great
communicative_purpose: To express that things are going well or that there's good fortune
trigger_situations: Good circumstances, positive situations, lucky moments
variations: c'est la belle, c'est la belle life, on a la belle, c'est beautiful
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'la belle' meaning 'the beautiful one' (feminine noun)
example_1: On a eu de la chance avec la météo, c'est la belle aujourd'hui.
example_2: Le nouveau job, c'est la belle ! L'équipe est incroyable.
example_3: Trop la chance, c'est la belle ce mois-ci !
example_1_translation: We were lucky with the weather, it's beautiful today.
example_2_translation: The new job is great! The team is incredible.
example_3_translation: So lucky, it's beautiful this month!
